original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Drug allergy: diagnosis and management — Recommendations
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE allergy, emergency, medicine, and referral adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: People of all ages with a possible or established drug allergy or suspected drug-related reaction.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary, emergency, inpatient, outpatient, and specialist-allergy documentation.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for documenting suspected-drug identity, indication, dose-count or exposure duration, route, reaction description, severity, timing, examination, and structured allergy record. It does not diagnose drug allergy, stop a medicine, generate treatment, or make a referral.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendation 1.1.1 and Tables 1–3 — assessment, reaction patterns, and timing
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting history, examination, timing after exposure, erythema, urticaria, angioedema, respiratory or cardiovascular features, macules, papules, fixed eruption, fever, lymphadenopathy, mucosal erosion, vesicles, blistering, detachment, pustules, photosensitivity, and vasculitic features without asserting a diagnosis.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.9 — recording and sharing drug-allergy information
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports structured recording of drug name, reaction signs, symptoms, severity, date and time, indication, number of doses or days before onset, route, status, and specialist-investigation results already available.

title: Drug allergy: diagnosis and management — Recommendations
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE allergy, emergency, medicine, and referral adaptation
population: People of all ages with a possible or established drug allergy or suspected drug-related reaction.
intended_setting: Primary, emergency, inpatient, outpatient, and specialist-allergy documentation.
